mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, driving positive forward S&P 500 returns as investors price in cheaper capital and higher present values of future cash flows.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 10.0% signals labor market stabilization and rising consumer income, which increases corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of months following UNRATE crossing below 10.0%, or t-statistic of return difference vs baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with >55% accuracy, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving factory utilization and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods when INDPRO increases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains pinned below 0.25%, quantitative easing expectations dominate pricing, causing equity markets to bid up valuations and generate positive forward returns regardless of near-term economic weakness.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market healing rather than statistical artifacts, which sustains consumer spending growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payout capacity, creating a mechanical upward pressure on equity prices that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive GDP growth coincides with negative market returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5% signals rapid capacity utilization recovery, which triggers algorithmic momentum strategies and short-term equity buying pressure over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months meeting the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
